description_long: This object is a long, slender polearm with a sharply pointed, double-edged spearhead at one end and a decorative, faceted pommel at the other. The shaft appears to be metallic, segmented by ornamental collars and rings, giving it a reinforced and slightly ornate appearance. The spearhead is symmetrical, tapering to a fine point, and features a subtle central ridge for added strength. The pommel is multi-faceted and geometric, likely designed for balance and grip. The overall finish is dark metallic, with some lighter highlights on the decorative elements. This weapon is reminiscent of ceremonial or high-quality spears used in historical or fantasy urban settings.
description: A long, double-edged spear with a pointed blade, reinforced metallic shaft, and a faceted decorative pommel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, double-edged spearhead, and segmented shaft with decorative collars.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the pointed spearhead and the narrow profile of the shaft.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the spearhead and shaft details, with the pommel visible at the far end.
description_view_3: View from the pommel end, showing the geometric pommel and the tapering shaft leading to the spearhead.
attribute_handle_design: straight metallic shaft with segmented decorative collars and a faceted pommel
attribute_ornamentation: geometric decorative collars and faceted pommel
